summary: Travel now mirrors the GUI gateway callback execution pattern and renders a full right-pane property_block from the itinerary agent's final_slot_state. This fixes the post-submit 404 caused by polling gateway /api/executions, which is not exposed on gateway.mestumre.dev, and closes the Tutorial 08 right-pane gap.

travel.title: fix(ui): use gateway callbacks and surface all trip slots

docs.title: docs(tutorials): note full travel slot pane

changes: Travel GraphQL execution now opens the authenticated gateway SSE stream, passes clientId into executePlaybook, and resolves from playbook/result callbacks.
Travel no longer relies on GET https://gateway.mestumre.dev/api/executions/{id} for authenticated production runs.
ChatThread accepts final callback payloads with data.render and data.final_slot_state.
RightPane renders Region, Dates, Party, Star rating, Budget, Bed type, and Amenities from final_slot_state using the existing property_block widget and edit-pencil rows.
Tutorial 08 now describes the full right-pane slot surface and removes the stale v1 limitation.
validation: repos/travel: npm run test (9 files, 19 tests)
travel Cloudflare Pages main deploy 25834193886 passed
docs Cloudflare Pages main deploy 25834194053 passed
browser_smoke: Submit: find ticket from sfo to NY next week
Expected: no generic Request failed with status code 404 from /api/executions polling
Expected: widget result appears from gateway SSE callback
Expected: right pane updates captured slots as the agent returns final_slot_state
notes: Kadyapam-side browser smoke remains the final user-visible gate because the live session token is browser-local.
The remaining extraction misspelling/normalization quality for inputs like 'NY next weeek' belongs to a future agent prompt/parser polish round if needed.
